annotations.label: Universal enveloping algebra (informal)

anchor.snippet: the universal enveloping algebra of a Lie algebra is the unital associative algebra whose representations correspond precisely to the representations of that Lie algebra

annotations.note: The universal enveloping algebra as an R-algebra is defined in Mathlib.Algebra.Lie.UniversalEnveloping.

annotations.label: Identification with left-invariant differential operators

anchor.snippet: may be identified with the algebra of left-invariant differential operators on the group

annotations.note: Mathlib defines the Lie algebra of left-invariant derivations on a Lie group but not its isomorphism with U(g).

annotations.label: Universal enveloping algebra as largest embedding

anchor.snippet: called the universal enveloping algebra of

annotations.note: This is Mathlib's `UniversalEnvelopingAlgebra R L`, characterised by the universal property `UniversalEnvelopingAlgebra.lift`.

annotations.label: U(g) via generators and relations

anchor.snippet: Then the universal enveloping algebra is the associative algebra (with identity) generated by elements

annotations.note: Mathlib builds U(g) as the tensor algebra quotiented by the Lie-compatibility relation `UniversalEnvelopingAlgebra.Rel`, i.e. by exactly this generators/relations presentation.

annotations.label: Universal enveloping algebra of sl(2,C)

anchor.snippet: Consider, for example, the Lie algebra sl(2,C)

annotations.note: Mathlib has `IsSl2Triple` characterising sl₂-triples in a Lie algebra but does not compute the enveloping algebra of sl(2,ℂ) explicitly.

annotations.label: PBW basis spans U(g) (preview)

anchor.snippet: asserts that these elements are linearly independent and thus form a basis for the universal enveloping algebra

annotations.note: No Poincaré–Birkhoff–Witt basis result appears in Mathlib (grep and loogle for `Birkhoff`/`PBW` return no hits).

annotations.label: Formal definition of U(g) as a quotient

anchor.snippet: is defined as the quotient space

annotations.note: `UniversalEnvelopingAlgebra R L` is literally the quotient of the tensor algebra by the ring congruence generated by `Rel.lie_compat`.

annotations.label: U for Lie superalgebras

anchor.snippet: The above is exactly how the universal enveloping algebra for Lie superalgebras is constructed

annotations.note: Mathlib does not have Lie superalgebras (grep for `LieSuperalgebra` returns no files).

annotations.label: Gerstenhaber algebra via exterior product

anchor.snippet: If the base algebra is a Lie algebra, then the result is the Gerstenhaber algebra

annotations.note: No Gerstenhaber algebra in Mathlib (grep returns no hits).

annotations.label: Universal property of U(g)

anchor.snippet: Then there exists a unique unital algebra homomorphism

annotations.note: `UniversalEnvelopingAlgebra.lift` gives the bijection between Lie-algebra maps `L →ₗ⁅R⁆ A` and algebra maps `U(L) →ₐ[R] A`, with uniqueness via `hom_ext`/`lift_unique`.

annotations.label: Poincaré–Birkhoff–Witt theorem (basis form)

anchor.snippet: The Poincaré–Birkhoff–Witt theorem then states that one can obtain a basis

annotations.note: Comments in `Mathlib.Algebra.Lie.Free` and `Mathlib.Algebra.Lie.SerreConstruction` explicitly note that PBW is not available in Mathlib.

annotations.label: PBW: U(g) and symmetric algebra are isomorphic vector spaces

anchor.snippet: This basis should be easily recognized as the basis of a symmetric algebra

annotations.note: Mathlib has `SymmetricAlgebra` but no vector-space isomorphism `U(g) ≃ S(g)` from PBW.

annotations.label: PBW theorem (coordinate-free)

anchor.snippet: one can view the Poincaré–Birkhoff–Witt theorem as stating that

annotations.note: No PBW statement (basis or filtered/graded form) exists in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Jordan algebra construction yields exterior algebra

anchor.snippet: The theorem, applied to Jordan algebras , yields the exterior algebra

annotations.note: Mathlib has Jordan algebras and exterior algebras separately but no PBW-style relation between them.

annotations.label: Left-invariant differential operators isomorphic to U(g)

anchor.snippet: is isomorphic to the universal enveloping algebra

annotations.note: Only the Lie algebra of left-invariant derivations is defined; its algebra of higher-order operators and the isomorphism with `UniversalEnvelopingAlgebra` are not.

annotations.label: Algebra of symbols

anchor.snippet: the algebra of symbols

annotations.note: No `algebra of symbols` construction on U(g) exists in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Heisenberg algebra and Weyl algebra (Moyal product)

anchor.snippet: The universal enveloping algebra of the Heisenberg algebra is the Weyl algebra

annotations.note: Neither the Heisenberg Lie algebra nor the Weyl algebra is defined in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Representations of g correspond to U(g)-modules

anchor.snippet: correspond in a one-to-one manner to the modules over

annotations.note: The infrastructure (`LieModule`, `UniversalEnvelopingAlgebra.lift` into `Module.End`) is present but the explicit equivalence between Lie modules and U(g)-modules is not stated as a theorem.

annotations.label: Kronecker product isomorphism

anchor.snippet: there is an isomorphism, known as the Kronecker product

annotations.note: No Hopf-algebra comultiplication/Kronecker product on `UniversalEnvelopingAlgebra` is defined in Mathlib.

annotations.label: U of free Lie algebra is free associative algebra

anchor.snippet: the universal enveloping algebra of a free Lie algebra is isomorphic to the free associative algebra

annotations.note: `FreeLieAlgebra.universalEnvelopingEquivFreeAlgebra` gives the algebra equivalence `U(FreeLieAlgebra R X) ≃ₐ[R] FreeAlgebra R X`.

annotations.label: Casimir operators as basis of center

anchor.snippet: the Casimir operators form a distinguished basis from the center

annotations.note: No `Casimir` declarations in Mathlib (grep and loogle both return zero hits).

annotations.label: Quadratic Casimir operator

anchor.snippet: the quadratic Casimir operator is

annotations.note: Mathlib has no Casimir operator construction.

annotations.label: Harish-Chandra isomorphism for center of U(g)

anchor.snippet: The center of the universal enveloping algebra of a simple Lie algebra is given in detail by the Harish-Chandra isomorphism

annotations.note: No Harish-Chandra isomorphism in Mathlib (grep returns no hits).

annotations.label: Number of independent Casimirs equals rank

anchor.snippet: The number of algebraically independent Casimir operators of a finite-dimensional semisimple Lie algebra is equal to the rank of that algebra

annotations.note: `LieAlgebra.rank` exists but no theorem relating it to the number of independent Casimirs (which are not defined).

annotations.label: Casimir for SO(3): squared angular momentum

anchor.snippet: The rotation group SO(3) is of rank one

annotations.note: No Casimir for SO(3) or angular-momentum-squared operator is defined in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Quadratic Casimir as Laplacian on Riemannian manifolds

anchor.snippet: For the quadratic Casimir invariant, this is the Laplacian

annotations.note: This link between Casimir operator and Laplace–Beltrami operator is not formalized.

annotations.label: Abelian Lie algebra: U(g) is polynomial algebra

anchor.snippet: is commutative; and if a basis of the vector space

annotations.note: Mathlib has `IsLieAbelian` and `MvPolynomial` but no isomorphism `U(g) ≃ MvPolynomial ι R` for abelian g.

annotations.label: Lie group case: U(g) as left-invariant differential operators

anchor.snippet: is the Lie algebra corresponding to the Lie group G

annotations.note: The Lie algebra of left-invariant derivations exists, but no realisation of U(g) as invariant differential operators.

annotations.label: U(g) as convolution algebra of distributions at identity

anchor.snippet: as the convolution algebra of distributions supported only at the identity element

annotations.note: No convolution algebra of point-supported distributions on a Lie group in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Weyl algebra from Heisenberg Lie algebra

anchor.snippet: The algebra of differential operators in n variables with polynomial coefficients may be obtained starting with the Lie algebra of the Heisenberg group

annotations.note: Neither the Weyl algebra nor the Heisenberg Lie algebra is defined in Mathlib.

annotations.label: U(g) is a filtered quadratic algebra

anchor.snippet: The universal enveloping algebra of a finite-dimensional Lie algebra is a filtered quadratic algebra

annotations.note: `QuadraticAlgebra` exists but no filtration or quadratic-algebra structure is put on `UniversalEnvelopingAlgebra`.

annotations.label: U(g) inherits Hopf algebra structure from tensor algebra

anchor.snippet: the tensor algebra has a Hopf algebra structure on it, and because the Lie bracket is consistent with (obeys the consistency conditions for) that Hopf structure, it is inherited by the universal enveloping algebra

annotations.note: `HopfAlgebra` is defined in Mathlib but no `HopfAlgebra` instance is placed on `UniversalEnvelopingAlgebra`.

annotations.label: Gelfand–Naimark for commutative Hopf algebras

anchor.snippet: the Gelfand–Naimark theorem essentially states that every commutative Hopf algebra is isomorphic to the Hopf algebra of continuous functions on some compact topological group

annotations.note: No Gelfand–Naimark theorem for commutative Hopf algebras exists in Mathlib (grep for `GelfandNaimark` returns no hits).
